mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market, which drives higher aggregate demand and corporate earnings, leading to positive equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.02 for a continuous 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near the zero lower bound, monetary policy has limited capacity to respond to negative shocks. This perceived lack of policy ammunition makes equities more vulnerable, leading to negative returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where retail sales (RSAFS) are accelerating while new housing starts (HOUST) are decelerating signals a weakening in long-term consumer confidence and investment despite current consumption. This forward-looking weakness predicts negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month change in RSAFS > 0 and 3-month change in HOUST < 0
failure_condition: The specified divergence pattern is not followed by statistically significant negative S&P 500 returns (t-test p > 0.10) over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment environment, a significant month-over-month decrease in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a robust recovery is underway, boosting investor sentiment and leading to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is above 6.0%, instances of a month-over-month decrease are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) reflects broad strength in the manufacturing and production sectors of the economy. This real activity strength translates quickly into positive market sentiment and near-term equity gains.
failure_condition: The correlation between 3-month changes in INDPRO and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically positive over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) while the central bank is constrained at the zero lower bound (FEDFUNDS) creates a negative outlook for equities. The market anticipates future aggressive tightening, which will increase discount rates and compress multiples.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 has a positive average 60-day return during periods that satisfy the trigger condition over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real GDP (GDPC1) growth below a critical threshold signals an impending recession. This fundamental weakness in aggregate demand leads to downward revisions in earnings expectations and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: In the quarters following a trigger event, the S&P 500 posts positive returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over a short period (e.g., 3 months) is a highly reliable real-time indicator of a recession. This shock to the labor market causes a sharp decline in equity prices as investors price in a severe downturn.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of UNRATE rises > 0.3pp above its 12-month low
failure_condition: The signal's precision (true negatives / (true negatives + false negatives)) in predicting negative 20-day returns falls below 0.55 in a walk-forward test.
